room_communications.message: Sergeant Halda Brakk taps the slate beside her. "If you need another pair of hands, ask me about recruits."

enlist: Tarn Rusk checks the knot on his sword belt. "I take the left side. You call the pace."
Tarn Rusk tests his blade in its sheath. "Ready when you are."
dismiss: "I will be at the muster fire," Tarn Rusk says. "Ask if the road turns bad."
victory: Tarn Rusk wipes his blade on a scrap of sacking and looks to the road ahead.
"Everyone whole?" Tarn Rusk asks before sheathing his sword.
Tarn Rusk checks the fallen for anything still moving.

enlist: Bram Keld settles the bronze-bound cudgel across one shoulder. "Stay behind me when the line closes."
dismiss: Bram Keld sets his cudgel beside the muster bench. "I will keep my place by the wall."
victory: Bram Keld looks over the others for wounds before lowering his cudgel.

enlist: Ora Pell closes her satchel and falls in beside you. "Show me the wound before it becomes a problem."
dismiss: Ora Pell returns her shears to the satchel. "Boil the bandages before you need them."
victory: Ora Pell checks the fallen, then reaches for a roll of linen.
"If you are bleeding, hold still," Ora Pell says, already opening her satchel.

room_communications.message: A scarred woman on the tower platform grins without warmth. "Climb all this way just to die at my boots? Good. Saves me the walk."
A thin man by the signal brazier laughs through broken teeth. "You smell rain-soaked. Let me fix that with fire."
A hooded killer rises from the crenels as if he had been nailed there by shadow. "Too slow," he says, already moving.
A broad priest in split vestments lifts a massive maul from the chapel stones. "Mercy has left this fort. Only weight remains."
The warlord in the keep-heart rises beneath his torn black banner and drags his greatsword free. "You broke my corners. Now break here, in the middle, where all things end."
Aurek's voice hardens. "You have made yourself an enemy of this order. Come no closer unless you mean to die for it."
Aurek says, "Well met, traveller. I have nothing more to offer just now."
Aurek says, "Hail! Well met traveller. I may have something for you. Come, listen to an old man."
Aurek says, "Welcome back. Are you ready to listen to my story?"
Aurek says, "Thank you for listening to an old man ramble."

keyword_actions.message: Aurek bares his teeth. "There will be no parley now. Defend yourself."
Aurek says, "Thank you for listening to an old man ramble."
Aurek says, "I have already entrusted my key to another. Return when the redoubt turns again."
Aurek says, "Paladins of our order first bow with respect."
Aurek bows in return and places a bronze key in your hand. "Take this to the weathered chest in the loft above the starting chamber."
keyword_actions.already_message: Aurek says, "I've already given you everything I can."

room_communications.message: Samiel squares his shoulders. "You have chosen treachery. Leave this walk or answer for it."
A veiled paladin hails you quietly. "Traveller, the blasphemer to the south was once our chaplain. If you have the strength, put him down and return."
Samiel bows his head. "The vault has gone still. Kneel at the broken sun and speak absolution; the lower tomb should answer now. If you find the sepulcher key below, bring it to me."
Samiel says, "You have done this redoubt a grace I will remember."

keyword_actions.message: Samiel keeps his sabre ready. "No counsel remains for oathbreakers."
Samiel says, "The thing to the south was once Brother Merovech, keeper of this vault. End him, and perhaps the old ways below will answer us again."
Samiel says, "Return to the vault and kneel at the broken sun. Speak absolution, and the lower sepulcher should open. Bring me the sepulcher key if you survive what waits below."

Samiel receives the sepulcher key with both hands and exhales at last. "Then the lower dead may sleep again. You have my thanks, and the order's."
You gain 450 experience.
keyword_actions.already_message: Samiel says, "The key is already where it belongs."

keyword_actions.message: Samiel shakes his head. "What I need is the sepulcher key from the tomb below, nothing less."
Samiel says, "You have done enough. Go with the sun's mercy."
